vitals.location: Cenes de la Vega, Granada, Spain

climate.description: Classic Sierra Nevada spring: warm valley starts (60-70°F) contrast cold high-altitude summits (30-50°F), potential snow even in May.
challenges: Precipitation: Moderate, with possible flurries at elevation.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 73.

course_description.character: Brutal high-altitude road gran fondo through Sierra Nevada's demanding alpine passes, blending endurance suffering with panoramic mountain beauty.
course_description.signature_challenge: The continuous 38km+ mountain passes culminating near Veleta peak at over 3100m, Europe's highest road finish line.
suffering_zones: Initial Ascent from Cenes de la Vega: Steep ramps out of the lowlands quickly sap early energy before the main Sierra Nevada walls.
Mid-Route Mountain Passes: Relentless climbs through Genil Pines and Las Sabinas Pass accumulate massive vertical.
Final Push to Pradollano Finish: Highest efforts near 2100-3100m altitude where thin air and fatigue collide.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Collaborations with TODOSIERRANEVADA for discounts; Granada city offers charming hotels minutes away.
logistics.transport: Efficiently organized with staggered starts (146km at 8AM, 114km at 9:30AM), bib pickup at Polideportivo Municipal de Cenes de la Vega, bag drop, bike wash, and finish services at Pradollano.

history.origin_story: Emblematic Spanish gran fondo showcasing Granada's cycling heritage with Gran Fondo and Subida al Veleta formats.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Spanish climbing crucible for masochists who crave thin air and endless VAM.
